title: Performance Analysis
subtitle: Long-term fitness trends, monthly performance, and correlations between average metrics.
fitnessAdvancedCaption: Training-load trend over time. CTL (Fitness) is the 42-day average load, reflecting long-term conditioning; ATL (Fatigue) is the 7-day average load, reflecting short-term fatigue; TSB (Form) = CTL − ATL, plotted on the right axis. Green stripes (TSB −20 → −5): {zoneOptimal}; blue stripes (TSB −5 → 10): {zoneRecovery}.
runComboCaption: Each bar is the average distance per run in that month (km, left axis). Lines show average pace, heart rate, cadence, running power, and elevation gain per session; some axes are hidden to keep the chart readable — hover the chart for exact values. Click a metric in the legend to show or hide it.
rideComboCaption: Each bar is the average distance per ride in that month (km, left axis). Lines show average power, speed, heart rate, cadence, and elevation gain per session; some axes are hidden to keep the chart readable — hover the chart for exact values. Click a metric in the legend to show or hide it.
correlationCaption: Each point is one activity, colored by year. Choose two metrics for the X and Y axes to examine the relationship between them — for example pace and heart rate, or cadence and power.
bestEffortCaptionPrefix: Fastest time each year over each distance, determined with a sliding window over raw GPS data — the same method as the
bestEffortCaptionSuffix: page. Activities without valid GPS data are excluded from the calculation.
